interested_parties.name: Data governance
interested_parties.stake: Added step, possible duplicate of security

current_state_summary: A user emails the service desk to request access to an application; a form is returned; the user selects roles; approvals run through manager -> application owner -> security (segregation-of-duties) -> a data-governance sign-off; an engineer then provisions access by hand in the target system. No periodic access review after the fact.

process_boundary.trigger_event: User needs access to an internal application
process_boundary.start_point: Access request email to the service desk
process_boundary.end_point: Access provisioned in the target system
process_boundary.reusable_pattern_fit: Likely a reusable 'request -> approve -> provision -> recertify' access pattern across many applications

pain_points: Manual, email-driven approvals sit in inboxes (slow)
~25% of requests ask for the wrong roles and bounce back
Over-requesting + manual provisioning leads to excess access (audit exposure)
No periodic access review after provisioning ('once you're in, you're in')
No single owner across service desk, app owners, security, and IT ops

approvers.purpose: Sign-off (purpose unclear - possible duplicate of security review)

desired_outcome: Access requests tied to predefined role bundles (prevents over-asking); automated segregation-of-duties checking; provisioning without manual keying; an auditable record. Cycle time cut from ~6 days to 1-2.

value_hypothesis.time_saved: ~4-5 business days per request; less rework from role bundles
value_hypothesis.cost_saved: Reduced manual provisioning effort across ~200 requests/month
value_hypothesis.quality_or_risk: Lower audit exposure via least-privilege role bundles + automated SoD checks + an auditable trail and recertification

sox_controls.control_area: Access controls / segregation of duties (IT general controls) over systems holding financial data

data_types: customer_pii (systems in scope hold personal data)

required_controls_to_confirm: Segregation-of-duties review must be preserved in any streamlined flow
An auditable approval + provisioning trail must be retained
Least-privilege enforcement (role bundles) must not weaken existing control

accountable_owner_candidate: none identified - needs assignment (spans service desk, app owners, security, and IT ops)
local_champion: Marco (IT ops lead) - willing to champion, but has no authority over security or app-owner teams

stakeholder_groups.impact: New self-service request via role bundles instead of free-text
Fewer, clearer approvals; some steps may be automated
SoD moves from manual review to automated checks + exceptions
Provisioning shifts from manual keying to automated with oversight

change_management.communications_needed: Explain to each group why the change helps them (less waiting, less rework, cleaner audit), not just that it's happening.
change_management.training_or_enablement_needed: Short enablement on requesting via role bundles; runbook for the automated SoD exceptions.
resistance_risks: Security may resist automating SoD without confidence in the checks
App owners may feel loss of control over their approval step
The data-governance sign-off owner may resist removing that step
change_management.handoff_plan: Transfer to the assigned accountable owner once the pilot proves out, with runbooks and the audit trail in place.
change_management.support_model_after_pilot: Defined support path for access issues + a recurring access-recertification cadence.

quick_win_flag.note: The data-governance sign-off appears to duplicate the security segregation-of-duties review and was added at an unknown point - candidate to question and possibly remove (only after confirming it provides no distinct required control).

urgency_note: No hard deadline, but an access-review audit is expected later this year and the manual trail will not hold up well.
open_questions: What distinct control, if any, does the data-governance sign-off provide beyond the security SoD review?
Exactly which applications are in scope, and which hold financial or personal data (drives control requirements)?
Is there any existing role-bundle / role-based-access model to build on, or is access purely free-text today?
What are the access-recertification requirements for the upcoming audit?
Who should be the single accountable owner across service desk, app owners, security, and IT ops?

review_audit.approval_notes: Auto-extracted draft for human review; confirm the flagged governance items and open questions before acting.

change_log.change: Initial draft extracted from intake transcript.
